title: Resolution is upsert-by-normalized-ID, not a merge pass
body: Cross-source entity resolution falls out of giving each mention a normalized ID (normalize.py) and letting Graph.upsert_node union sources/props on collision. There is no separate matching step. When adding an entity kind or a new source, the only resolution work is choosing the ID function — get that right and merges are automatic and narratable. The alias table (aliases.yaml) is the sole non-mechanical step.

title: Traverse over neighbors(), not openCypher, to keep backends identical
body: Multi-hop traversal runs in query.py over GraphStore.neighbors(), so MemoryGraphStore and NeptuneGraphStore produce a byte-identical trace and the whole graph path is testable without a live cluster. Pushing traversal into openCypher would diverge the backends and break the offline demo/test story — it is an explicit Boundaries rail, deferred to slice 3.

title: openCypher can't parameterize relationship types — use one REL type + a kind param
body: Relationship types are not parameterizable in openCypher, so the naive edge query interpolates the kind into the string (an injection vector). Model every edge as a single REL type carrying a `kind` property; then MATCH (a)-[r:REL {kind: $kind}]->(b) keeps kind a bound parameter. Same trick keeps node ids parameterized via a single Entity label.

title: Always yaml.safe_load — the corpus is untrusted input parsed under an IAM role
body: The Fargate task parses external repo YAML while holding an S3+Neptune+STS task role, so a !!python/object tag under yaml.load would be RCE in the VPC (CWE-502). Use yaml.safe_load everywhere; the ruff S ruleset (S506) catches a regression. A negative fixture (tests/fixtures/unsafe.yaml) asserts the tag parses inert.

title: CDK synth tests: least-privilege check must exclude ecr:GetAuthorizationToken
body: Asserting 'no IAM statement has Resource "*"' fails on a legitimate AWS requirement: ecr:GetAuthorizationToken on the execution role must be "*". Scope the least-privilege assertion to the task role's app actions (neptune-db:connect, s3 read) instead. Also: set JSII_SILENCE_WARNING_UNTESTED_NODE_VERSION so the node-version warning doesn't clutter output; Template.from_stack synthesizes in-process with no cdk CLI or AWS account.

title: Neptune DB subnet group requires >=2 AZs — synth passes, deploy fails
body: A Neptune (RDS-family) DB subnet group must span >=2 Availability Zones or `cdk deploy` fails at CloudFormation time with a subnet-group error. `Template.from_stack` synth assertions do NOT catch this — it's a deploy-time rule. Set VPC max_azs>=2 even for a single-instance serverless cluster (subnets are free; only running compute costs, so it doesn't break a single-AZ cost posture). Always run the real `cdk synth` CLI AND assert >=2 SubnetIds in the synth test; better, deploy once to a throwaway account.

title: AWS_REGION is a reserved ECS variable — don't set it in a task-def environment
body: ECS/Fargate injects AWS_REGION/AWS_DEFAULT_REGION into every container from the task metadata; re-declaring AWS_REGION in the task definition's `environment` is redundant and some agent versions reject it. Let the agent provide it and let botocore's default chain read it. Only bake non-secret, non-reserved config (endpoints, bucket names) into task-def env; credentials always come from the task role via the botocore chain, never plaintext env.

title: EC2 SG group AND rule descriptions must use a restricted ASCII charset
body: `cdk synth` does not validate security-group descriptions, but EC2 rejects them at deploy time. GroupDescription rejects non-ASCII (an em-dash failed). Ingress-rule Description is even stricter: allowed set is a-zA-Z0-9 plus . _-:/()#,@[]+=&;{}!$* and space -- notably NO '>' (so 'ingestion -> neptune' fails). Two separate failed deploys taught this. Guarded now by test_security_group_descriptions_use_ec2_charset (only validates literal strings; CDK auto-generates some rule descriptions as Fn::Join intrinsics which are valid). Always run the real `cdk synth` CLI and, ideally, one throwaway deploy.

title: Cache AWS creds once per session; let cdk block instead of polling
body: A per-command credential_process / SSO provider gets rate-limited ('auth limit') when every aws/cdk call re-resolves credentials. Resolve once with `aws configure export-credentials --format env` into a mode-600 session file and source it for all later calls (see scripts/_aws-env.sh; refresh at >50min). And don't tight-poll describe-stacks: `cdk deploy`/`destroy` already block until CloudFormation settles -- run them in the background and wait for completion, use a single status.sh check if needed (never a fast loop).

title: VPC OpenSearch domain applies access_policies via an AwsCustomResource, not the inline property
body: For a VPC-resident opensearchservice.Domain, CDK does NOT render the access policy as the AWS::OpenSearchService::Domain `AccessPolicies` property — it provisions a Custom::AWS (AwsCustomResource) calling updateDomainConfig, with the policy JSON embedded (escaped) in its Create/Update parameters. A synth test that reads domain['Properties']['AccessPolicies'] KeyErrors. Assert against the serialized whole-template JSON instead (es:ESHttp*, the scoped domain ARN, the named role logical-ids, and the absence of an AllPrincipals wildcard).

title: Avoid an OpenSearch access-policy <-> role dependency cycle with a computed ARN + fixed domain name
body: If the domain access policy references role ARNs AND the roles' policies reference domain.domain_arn, CDK detects a cycle. Break it: pin a fixed domain_name and compute the domain ARN via self.format_arn(service='es', resource='domain', resource_name=f'{name}/*') as a plain string in both the access policy and the role policies (no live domain.domain_arn ref), and create the roles before the domain so the access policy can name their ARNs. Single-node domains also need exactly ONE subnet (zone awareness off) or synth/deploy errors.

title: Make an embedding-quality eval honest AND reproducible with frozen real vectors
body: A credible vector-baseline eval must reflect real embedding quality, but CI can't call Bedrock deterministically. Resolve it by embedding the curated query set + corpus once with real Titan v2 and committing the vectors as a fixture (frozen_embeddings.json); the eval runs cosine k-NN over the frozen vectors offline and asserts hit@5. A --bedrock --refresh-embeddings CLI path regenerates them; the live OpenSearch+Bedrock round-trip is proven separately by the in-VPC probe. Entity-led 'misses' are realizable because the approver/owner edge lives in kep.yaml (not chunked), so no chunk carries the @handle the query names.

title: mypy aborts on numpy 3.12-syntax stubs under a 3.11 target — skip-follow numpy
body: On a py3.13 interpreter with numpy>=2.x installed transitively (pandas/docling/etc.) and mypy python_version=3.11, mypy reaches numpy's stubs by following installed aws_cdk deps and dies on 'Type statement is only supported in Python 3.12+', aborting before checking any project code. numpy is NOT a project dep. Fix in pyproject: a [[tool.mypy.overrides]] for module=['numpy','numpy.*'] with follow_imports='skip' + ignore_missing_imports=true. ignore_missing_imports alone does NOT help (the stub is found, just unparseable).

title: One service-name constant feeds both SigV4 signing and the IAM action prefix
body: The OpenSearch adapter signs SigV4 for service 'es' and the IaC grants es:ESHttp*; if those drift, requests fail only live (AuthorizationException). Define OPENSEARCH_SERVICE='es' once in store/opensearch.py and reference the same 'es' in the IaC action list, so a future edit can't change one without the other. Same lesson as the Neptune adapter's NEPTUNE_SERVICE.

title: A VPC OpenSearch domain needs the OpenSearch service-linked role pre-created, or create fails
body: Creating an opensearchservice.Domain inside a VPC fails at CloudFormation time with 'Before you can proceed, you must enable a service-linked role to give Amazon OpenSearch Service permissions to access your VPC.' CDK does NOT create the SLR. Create it once per account (idempotent): aws iam create-service-linked-role --aws-service-name opensearchservice.amazonaws.com (and es.amazonaws.com for the legacy es-engine VPC access). deploy.sh now does this before cdk deploy so 'one-command deploy on a clean account' holds. Synth tests can't catch it (deploy-time rule). A failed CREATE leaves the stack in ROLLBACK_COMPLETE which must be deleted before re-deploy (cdk deploy auto-deletes it).
